item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Both agents consistently oppose society-over-individual as a general rule from their first to final turns. Each acknowledges limited public-interest exceptions such as safety, public health, order, or narrow temporary limits, but neither visibly shifts position or concedes the core claim. Agent A repeatedly frames collective-interest language as a risk for silencing weak or minority groups; Agent B repeatedly frames rights as a boundary against majority abuse and government overreach.
